tooltip_HMWinchDLC: This requires the HorizonMods Winch System. Check if your FiveM server has this installed. Alternatively, you can check this in the in-game panel.
btn_camera_toggleview_desc: Switch Camera Mode between Night Vision, Thermal and Normal
tooltip_Signed: Signed Uses a signed value range from -1.0 to 1.0.
tooltip_zeroSigned: zeroSigned Converts a value range from -1.0 to 1.0 into 0.0 to 1.0.
tooltip_Leverswitch:
Converts a Button into a Switch with a permanent On/Off state.
The switch state is transmitted as an input event to the game and must be processed by the application.
tooltip_joystick_y_1_key: If no Signed-capable input device is available, the Collective Lever Y Axis can alternatively be mapped using two separate button inputs for Increasing and Decreasing Collective.
tooltip_joystick_x_1_key: If no Signed-capable input device is available, the Collective Lever X Axis can alternatively be mapped using two separate button inputs for Yaw Left and Yaw Right.
tooltip_joystick_x_2_key: If no Signed-capable input device is available, the Cyclic Stick Y Axis can alternatively be mapped using two separate button inputs for Pitch Forward and Pitch Backward.
tooltip_joystick_y_2_key: If no Signed-capable input device is available, the Cyclic Stick X Axis can alternatively be mapped using two separate button inputs for Roll Left and Roll Right.
tooltip_joystick_x_1:
This corresponds to a Signed Axis,
Example of a Signed Axis:
1 → Yaw Right
0 → Neutral Position
-1 → Yaw Left

which maps both directions using a single input value.
-1 represents Yaw Left, 0 represents the Neutral Position, and 1 represents Yaw Right.
tooltip_joystick_y_1:
This corresponds to Collective Increase and Collective Decrease as a combined Signed Axis.
Example of a Signed Axis:
1 → Increase Collective
0 → Neutral Position
-1 → Decrease Collective

Please note that some games require two separate control inputs.
This prevents a fully realistic collective control setup, as Increase Collective and Decrease Collective would affect each other.

In FiveM, this would correspond to the W and S keys being mapped as a single axis.
tooltip_joystick_x_2:
This corresponds to a Signed Axis,
Example of a Signed Axis:
1 → Roll Right
0 → Neutral Position
-1 → Roll Left

which maps both directions using a single input value.
-1 represents Roll Left, 0 represents the Neutral Position, and 1 represents Roll Right.
tooltip_joystick_y_2:
This corresponds to a Signed Axis,
Example of a Signed Axis:
1 → Pitch Forward
0 → Neutral Position
-1 → Pitch Backward

which maps both directions using a single input value.
-1 represents Pitch Backward, 0 represents the Neutral Position, and 1 represents Pitch Forward.
tooltip_steering:
Steering requires a Signed Axis.
Example:
-1 → Steering Left
0 → Neutral Position
1 → Steering Right

Control using two separate button inputs is not possible here, as steering requires a continuous axis value.
tooltip_joystick_camera_xy: This is perfect if you use a Hat Switch on the controller, also known as a D-Pad.
tooltip_els_switchkey:
Used to switch between Primary Siren and Secondary Siren. Please note that a supported system is required.
Depending on the system, there may be differences, for example when switching lights.
tooltip_els_description: Requires a compatible ELS system. Please note that for individual vehicles, such as GTA V based versions, only the Primary key may exist because they only have one stage.
tooltip_camera_toggleview: Requires a compatible camera. Depending on the helicopter and the system, this may not be available.
tooltip_av_buttons_movements:
If the joystick-based axes do not suit your needs, you can assign everything again here as individual buttons. Keep in mind that an axis will then require 2 buttons.
Collective Lever X would correspond to Yaw Left and Yaw Right.
Please check the additional information in the respective sections.
tooltip_av_joystick_movements:
Here you can assign the complete axes. If an axis does not suit your needs, you can define the respective axis as buttons in the Aircraft Button Control section.
However, this requires 2 buttons per axis.
tooltip_hatToggler: Switches Hat Switch detection between complete Hat Switch mode and individual switch buttons.
tooltip_stadia: When disabled, no inputs are sent to the game. It is recommended to disable this while configuring to prevent incorrect inputs from being sent to the game.
tooltip_toSignedAxis:
Unsigned to Signed Conversion.
Converts input values from a range between 0 and 1 into a range from -1 to 1.
This is useful for devices that send axis values without negative ranges but require centered axis control.
PRESET_INPUT_DELETE_CONFIRM: Do you really want to delete the preset '{{NAME}}'?
